channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: AlteredBlood+ is a movement shooter set at the end of the universe where the key to survival is soaking up your enemies' blood
item.content:encoded:
The year is 3232 CE, a "self-replicating megastructure" named MOTHER is launched into the centre of space. Fast forward to the year 4,199,245,344,263,242,348,389,893,582,349,373 CE (no I'm not joking), and MOTHER has "consumed 99.99999997% of the Observable Universe." What's left of humanity hangs on in that tiny percentage that hasn't be consumed, which is where you, a Reaper, a creation of MOTHER's, steps in as part of AlteredBlood+, a movement-FPS platformer where the blood of your enemies is your strength. Also you're a furry!

Right, quite the setup there for this one. As big and bold as its concept is, a movement shooter lives by the finesse of its, ya know, movement. Quite convenient then that there's a demo to try out. AlteredBlood+ is a game that from its narrative to how it plays you can easily describe it as "a lot." You are constantly on the move so as to avoid incoming enemy attacks, with a pretty swish little arsenal to defend yourself with.

At its base you have a knife that can do a few things. The first is, you guessed it, a slicing attack that'll take out an enemy lickety split, sort of dashing you towards them if you're close enough. If you've got enough charge, a slice will send out a wave of energy that'll kill your enemies too. Time it just right, charge or not, your knife can also reflect your enemies' attacks. Somewhere along the way you'll pick up some guns, each appropriately chunky and boofy.

Let's touch base back on the blood. Oddly, blood in this game is more like sap, black, tar-like goo that brings out your best side. Your weapons gain special effects while standing, soaking in the sap, and your movement abilities are stronger too, allowing you to jump to much higher heights. It's a strong incentive to kill as many enemies as you can as quickly as you can, and to throw yourself head-first into danger; risk (dying), and reward (surviving, progressing).

I quite like what AlteredBlood+ is putting down, and I'm keen to see more of what its out there story is offering up too. You can wishlist it and try the demo out on Steam right now.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/alteredblood-is-a-movement-shooter-set-at-the-end-of-the-universe-where-the-key-to-survival-is-soaking-up-your-enemies-blood
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Paranormal Activity: Threshold is as dead as its ghosts, as the game gets canned because Paramount couldn't wait any longer
item.content:encoded:
"The game needed more time to ensure it was the best it could possibly be"

Sorry to all the Paranormal Activity heads out there, but the upcoming game adaptation Threshold has crossed over to the other side. Brian Clarke, the solo dev that goes by DarkStone Digital for his gaming exploits and maker of 2022's The Mortuary Assistant, shared the news to his personal Zwitter account earlier this week alongside a statement explaining why the game has been cancelled.

"It's no secret that game development means constant change and challenges especially as a solo indie," Clarke explains. "It became clear that the game needed more time to ensure it was the best it could possibly be. DreadXP stood by my need for more time and, together, we made a request to Paramount for a time extension. Sadly, Paramount did not agree to extend development."

I have a very important announcement everyone. Please have a read. Thank you all for your ongoing support of my work and love for indie games as a whole. 🖤🖤 pic.twitter.com/kmCS3t3BTq

— Brian Clarke ⚰️ (@DSDigitalDev) May 7, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

Clarke goes on to share that this put him in a position of having to make a choice between rushing through development and releasing a "sub-par product I would not be proud of," or asking for himself and Paramount to go their separate ways. "I chose the latter," he writes, while also making sure to mention again how DreadXP stood by him, and that the split from Paramount was amicable. "It was an honor to even be considered to work on an IP I love so much."

I admit to having felt quite apprehensive about a game adaptation of Paranormal Activity, namely because of how the tactile quality of recording on tape could have gotten lost in translation. I'll never get to have my apprehension proven wrong, or right, unfortunately. Its cancellation also makes me think of how awkward game development is compared to film; sure, a movie might get announced and never made, but how often do we actually get to see something from one only for it to be ultimately cancelled? I'm sure that isn't a broader concern in the games industry! Not one bit!



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/paranormal-activity-threshold-is-as-dead-as-its-ghosts-as-the-game-gets-canned-because-paramount-couldnt-wait-any-longer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Europa Universalis 5's latest patch notes took up 72 pages on a Google Doc so I think it might be a big one
item.content:encoded:
Plus, the game's first DLC is out now too

The latest patch for Europa Universalis 5 dropped earlier this week. Numbered 1.2 and titled Echinades, it is the game's "biggest update yet," as true a sounding statement as can be given that the patch notes come out to apparently 72 pages in a Google Doc. I don't have time to read all of that! So I won't, but I will tell you about the highlights kindly outlined by Paradox Tinto that feature in this update.

For this update, the Balkans have seen some major additions. There are more than 300 new advances and 150 new Dynamic Historical Events, alongside "extensive new content for Greece and the Balkans, a couple new formables, reforms, and privileges," according to this Steam blog post. There's plenty of bits that have had a big overhaul too.

The economy, for one, has had its trade route logic and Maritime Presence mechanics updated, alongside the addition of a "new Trade Orders feature, Urban Rights, the Megalopolis location rank, and some tweaks to hopefully reflect a more historically accurate economic setups." On the orthodoxy side of things, patriarchs are now characters, with the Law and Tenet system having been reworked and "Religious Influence fully replacing Rite Power."

Beyond that, the Holy Roman Empire has also seen an overhaul with a new Imperial Diet, UI rework and Imperial armies, not to mention a new Papal Authority system and "AI Country Personalities and Disposition Systems." Plus, military systems have been improved with refreshed logistics and more. You'll also find a new in-game event viewer, a sans-serif font accessibility option, 3D City Walls visuals, 2000 bug fixes, and modding support. Yeesh! That's a lot, and I didn't even read the full patch notes.

This update also brought with it the game's first big DLC which is all about saving the crumbling Byzantine Empire. Lot's to be getting on with, it seems!



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/europa-universalis-5s-latest-patch-notes-took-up-72-pages-on-a-google-doc-so-i-think-it-might-be-a-big-one
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Psychonauts developer Double Fine are the latest Microsoft-owned studio to unionise
item.content:encoded:
Some good news for the day: indie developer behind the cult classic psychic platformer Psychonauts and more Double Fine are unionising. As spotted by Aftermath, a petition was filed by the Microsoft-owned studio to the National Labor Relations board on May 7th, and are doing so with the Communications Workers of America (CWA), following suit from a number of other developers owned by the tech giant.

"On May 7, the workers at Microsoft studio Double Fine Productions announced their decision to form a union with CWA to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life," CWA said to Aftermath in a statement.

"In tandem with requesting voluntary recognition from the company, workers have also filed an election petition with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to secure union representation. We appreciate that Microsoft has taken a neutral approach and agreed not to interfere in any way with worker’s rights to organize unions." Also worth noting is that the union will include all "regular part-time and full-time employees," for a total of 42 staff members.

Not that there's ever a bad time to unionise, but it's been particularly important in recent years given that Microsoft has laid off thousands of workers since 2023 alone. Along the way, World of Warcraft and other Blizzard devs, workers at Doom studio Id Software, Call of Duty QA staff, and Zenimax QA workers have all formed unions of their own by working with the CWA. Now it would just be nice if all of these studios weren't owned by a company currently subject to a boycott.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/psychonauts-developer-double-fine-are-the-latest-microsoft-owned-studio-to-unionise
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
